rationale: Fingerprint is empty — source body is almost always a try/catch that returns `[]` on any parse failure (arduino-cli board list, etc.). This bucket has 6 distinct JS sources across 50 generators; no single Rust pipeline matches them all on real-world input (each variant reads different JSON paths). Strategy: feed inputs that all JS variants route through their catch / empty-filter branches — `"[]"` makes `JSON.parse("[]").filter(...) = []` and `.map(...) = []`; `""` and garbage strings throw inside `JSON.parse`, caught, return `[]`. Rust `split_lines+filter_empty+json_extract(sentinel)` also emits `[]` for these inputs (no object has the sentinel key). Happy-path fixtures for this bucket await the proposed `json_array_extract` transform.
